### Assistant

Implemented and committed the confirm modal unification.

Commit: `78105d63d` (`Use compact native confirm popup`)  
Goal usage: `268,936` tokens, about `22m 4s`.

Changed files:
- [src/confirm/parent_dialog.rs](/Users/johnlindquist/dev/script-kit-gpui/src/confirm/parent_dialog.rs)
- [src/confirm/window.rs](/Users/johnlindquist/dev/script-kit-gpui/src/confirm/window.rs)

What changed:
- Async destructive confirms now open the native parent-attached confirm popup directly instead of routing through the old in-window `ConfirmPrompt` path.
- Confirm popup bounds are compact, centered over the parent, capped at shortcut-modal-like width, and styled with the shared overlay animation/panel treatment.
- Focus state is synchronized through the native popup lifecycle, including Tab/Shift+Tab source coverage.
- NSWindow matching now includes position and size to avoid compact-popup collisions.

Verification:
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --lib confirm_window -- --nocapture`
- `SCRIPT_KIT_AGENT_TARGET_MODE=exclusive SCRIPT_KIT_AGENT_ID=modal-unify-0521 ./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --lib async_confirm_route_no_longer_uses_in_window_confirm_prompt_router -- --nocapture`
- `SCRIPT_KIT_AGENT_TARGET_MODE=exclusive SCRIPT_KIT_AGENT_ID=modal-unify-0521 ./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --lib confirm_focus_global_state_tracks_native_popup_focus_changes -- --nocapture`
- `SCRIPT_KIT_AGENT_TARGET_MODE=exclusive SCRIPT_KIT_AGENT_ID=modal-unify-0521 ./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --lib confirm_nswindow_search_matches_position_and_size -- --nocapture`
- `SCRIPT_KIT_AGENT_TARGET_MODE=exclusive SCRIPT_KIT_AGENT_ID=modal-unify-0521 ./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh check --lib`
- `SCRIPT_KIT_AGENT_TARGET_MODE=exclusive SCRIPT_KIT_AGENT_ID=modal-unify-0521 ./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh build --bin script-kit-gpui`
- Runtime proof path: `/tmp/sk-agentic-sessions/modal-unify-0521/app.log`
  - Confirm popup opened as `confirm-popup`
  - Parent resolved to `main`
  - Bounds logged as centered compact popup: `width=360px height=132px`
  - Elements snapshot returned `focused_semantic_id=button:0:confirm`

Skipped / partial verification:
- Full destructive caller flows like Quit Script Kit, Delete Note, clipboard/file/chat/builtin were not all executed end-to-end because they are real destructive paths; the shared async confirm funnel and SDK confirm path were verified instead.
- Runtime Tab proof was attempted, but DevTools `simulateKey` routed to `ScriptList`/ACP instead of the `confirm-popup`; I classified that as DevTools targeting/misrouting, not a product failure. Focus/key behavior is covered by focused source tests.
- Repo-wide `fmt --check` initially saw unrelated formatting drift; the commit hook passed formatting for the staged owned files.

Cleanup:
- Stopped `modal-unify-0521`.
- `session.sh status modal-unify-0521` reports `not_found` / `alive=false`.
- No matching `modal-unify-0521` process remains.
- Worktree still has unrelated dirty files from other work; they were not staged or committed.

If you need broad unrelated refactors or destructive operations, stop and report first.

## Round 15

### You

/goal Fix `;snippet` markdown metadata rendering so saved snippets use clean key/value metadata fences, not JSON objects.

User bug report / expected behavior:
- Current generated snippet example is wrong:
  ````markdown
  ## email

  ````metadata
  {
    "name": "email",
    "keyword": "@gma",
    "tool": "paste"
  }
  ````

  ````paste
  johnlindquist@gmail.com
  ````
  ````
- It should match the clean key/value style:
  ```markdown
  ## Hi to Hello

  ```metadata
  keyword: yay!
  description: Expand hi! to hello!
  ```

  ```paste
  hello!
  ```
  ```
- Fix the `;snippet` task only: metadata fences in `/Users/johnlindquist/.scriptkit/plugins/main/scriptlets/snippets.md` should render clean key/value pairs, not JSON.

Hard requirements:
- Read AGENTS.md and relevant repo-local skills before editing.
- This is a real `$parallel-goal-oracle-devtools` implementation worker, not read-only research.
- Use the required loop: gather current source/runtime data, consult Oracle before implementation, read the full Oracle output, implement, verify, commit, and clean up.
- Use Oracle slug `snippet-metadata-kv-0521` and session/process slug `snippet-metadata-kv-0521`.
- Preserve unrelated dirty work. Current known unrelated untracked files may include `memories/conversations/*`; do not touch them.
- Keep the change narrowly scoped. Likely owners are `src/scriptlets/snippet_markdown_store.rs` and focused tests, but inspect current source first because `;link` recently added adjacent markdown store code.
- Do not regress `;link` markdown behavior unless source proves it shares the same renderer and should also use key/value style for snippet-compatible metadata.

Verification expectations:
- Add or update focused tests proving snippet markdown writes metadata as key/value lines, including strings such as `keyword: @gma` and `description: ...`, and not JSON braces/quoted JSON keys.
- Keep existing snippet load/update/delete behavior working.
- Run focused tests through `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh`, plus the smallest broader check that can fail for the touched behavior.
- If runtime DevTools proof is practical, verify a `;snippet` create writes clean key/value metadata. If blocked by known destructive-submit/stale-runtime issues, classify precisely and provide strong focused source/test proof.
- Commit only the verified owned files and report commit hash, proof, skipped checks, and cleanup status.

## Round 16

### You

Start a separate $parallel-goal-oracle-devtools to tackle adding a scrollbar and proper scroll positioning to the the ;snippet view. Currently when you tab through, no scrollbar appears and the scoll positioning feels very unnatural. Add remove these pills in all the ;handler views [Image #1] , instead, add a * to the required fields

## Round 17

### You

/goal Fix `;snippet` / menu-syntax capture form scrolling and required-field presentation.

User report:
- In the `;snippet` capture/form view, tabbing through fields has unnatural scroll positioning.
- No scrollbar appears even when the form content overflows.
- The status/requirement pills shown near the header, like `; capture`, `needs body`, and `needs name or selected snippet`, should be removed in all `;handler`/menu-syntax capture views.
- Instead, indicate required fields by adding a `*` to the required field labels.
- Screenshot context shows the `;snippet` capture form with pills above fields and required badges on field cards.

Hard requirements:
- Read AGENTS.md and relevant repo-local skills before editing. Likely skills: `prompt-runtime`, `main-menu-search-selection`, `keyboard-focus-routing`, `gpui-ui-foundation`, `script-kit-devtools`, `testing-quality-gates`.
- This is a real `$parallel-goal-oracle-devtools` implementation worker, not read-only research.
- Use the required loop: gather current runtime/source data, consult Oracle before implementation, read full Oracle output, implement, verify, commit, cleanup.
- Use Oracle/session slug `snippet-form-scroll-required-0521`.
- Preserve unrelated dirty work. Another worker may be editing the snippet markdown metadata path (`snippet-metadata-kv-0521`), likely around `src/scriptlets/snippet_markdown_store.rs`; do not touch its files unless absolutely required and report first.
- Inspect `git status --short --untracked-files=all` before editing.
- Keep edits narrowly scoped to the form/view/rendering/scroll behavior and focused tests/contracts.

Functional target:
- Overflowing menu-syntax capture/form views show a usable scrollbar.
- Tabbing through fields keeps the focused field in a natural visible position, not jumpy or hidden.
- Remove the header requirement/status pills from all relevant `;handler` / menu-syntax capture form views.
- Required fields should be indicated by `*` in the field label, e.g. `Snippet *`, `name *`, instead of separate pills/badges.
- Preserve keyboard navigation and existing form semantics.

Verification expectations:
- Focused source tests/contracts for required-label rendering and absence of requirement pills if existing test surface supports it.
- Runtime/DevTools proof for `;snippet` form: enough fields to overflow, scrollbar/scroll state visible or measurable, Tab advances focus and keeps focused field visible naturally.
- If DevTools cannot directly expose scrollbar metrics, capture screenshot/state receipts and classify limitations precisely.
- Run all checks through `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh`.
- Commit only verified owned files and report commit hash, proof paths, skipped verification, and cleanup status.

Both follow-up workers completed and committed.

- `2b154f161` `Render snippet metadata as key value markdown`
  - `;snippet` now writes clean metadata fences like `keyword: @gma`.
  - Existing JSON metadata still loads and is rewritten cleanly on update.
  - Verified with `snippet_markdown_store`, `check --lib`, and diff check.

- `d69302f2b` `Fix capture form required labels and scroll reveal`
  - Removed the header requirement/status pills from capture form state.
  - Required fields now show `*` in labels, e.g. `Snippet *`, `name *`.
  - Removed the separate `required` badge chrome.
  - Tab reveal now keeps focused fields visible without top-align jumping.
  - Verified with source audit, `check`, and DevTools layout/state receipts in `target-agent/proofs/snippet-form-scroll-required-0521/`.

Final sweep: tracked worktree is clean. Only unrelated untracked `memories/conversations/*` files remain, plus the persistent `dev-watch` session/app process.

## Round 19

### You

$parallel-goal-oracle-devtools  there's a bug in the ;snippet form where the font size of the placeholder/fields increases when focused. Please keep the font a consistent smaller size and make sure it's part of the theme

## Round 20

### You

/goal Fix the `;snippet` capture/form field typography bug: placeholder and field font size increases when a field is focused. Keep font size consistent, smaller, and theme-owned.

User report:
- In the `;snippet` form, the placeholder/field text grows when focused.
- It should stay a consistent smaller size across focused and unfocused states.
- The size/style should come from the app theme/design tokens rather than being an ad hoc focus override.

Hard requirements:
- Read AGENTS.md and relevant repo-local skills before editing. Likely skills: `script-kit-devtools`, `gpui-ui-foundation`, `theme-config-preferences`, `prompt-runtime`, `keyboard-focus-routing`, and `testing-quality-gates`.
- This is a real `$parallel-goal-oracle-devtools` implementation worker, not read-only research.
- Use the required loop: gather current runtime/source data, consult Oracle before implementation, read full Oracle output, implement, verify, commit, cleanup.
- Use Oracle/session slug `snippet-form-font-size-0521`.
- Inspect `git status --short --untracked-files=all` before editing. Known unrelated untracked files may be `memories/conversations/*`; do not touch them.
- Preserve recent commits around `;snippet` form required labels/scroll reveal and metadata key/value rendering.
- Keep edits narrow. Likely owners are the capture form renderer / input styling / theme token path, but verify current source first.
- Use `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh` for all cargo invocations; do not use bare cargo.

Functional target:
- In `;snippet`, focused and unfocused form fields use the same smaller text size for placeholder/value text.
- Focus styling may still change border/background/accent, but must not change text size or cause layout jump.
- Typography should be theme-owned. If a suitable theme token already exists, use it. If not, add the smallest appropriate token/helper in the existing theme/style pattern and use it consistently.
- Scope the fix to menu-syntax/capture form fields unless source proves a shared input style is the correct owner.

Verification expectations:
- DevTools red data before implementation: capture state/layout around `;snippet`, focus at least two fields, record field/input text sizes or measurable layout deltas if available; screenshot/layout receipt if direct font metrics are not exposed.
- Oracle planning before edits using the red receipts and relevant source/skill context.
- Source tests/audits for no focused font-size override and theme-owned form field typography if existing test surface supports it.
- Runtime green proof after implementation: `;snippet` form focused/unfocused field placeholder/value sizes remain consistent, no visible text growth/layout jump when tabbing/focusing.
- Run focused checks via `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh`, plus `git diff --check`.
- Commit only verified owned files and report commit hash, proof paths, skipped checks, and cleanup status.

#### Tool 54 — Shell

```sh
cat > /tmp/snippet_evidence.md <<'EOF'
# Compact Evidence Supplement

## Prior-State Ledger
- prior_oracle_session: none

## User Goal
Fix `;snippet` capture/form field typography bug: placeholder and field font size increases when a field is focused. ## Round 22

### You

/goal Implement multiline support for the main `Snippet` field in the `;snippet` capture form, audit other menu-syntax form fields that should be multiline, and remove duplicated `key:` prefixes from field placeholders.

User report / requirements:
- The main `Snippet` field of the `;snippet` form needs multiline input, e.g. for email bodies or longer snippets.
- The only existing multiline component the user knows about is the agent chat main input; inspect/reuse the appropriate existing component/pattern rather than inventing ad hoc behavior.
- Audit other menu-syntax/capture forms for fields that should be multiline and implement the needed ones in the same coherent pattern if in scope.
- In field placeholders, remove duplicated key prefixes like `name:` / `keyword:` / `description:`. The key already appears in the label above the field.

Hard requirements:
- Read AGENTS.md and relevant repo-local skills before editing. Likely skills: `script-kit-devtools`, `prompt-runtime`, `gpui-ui-foundation`, `keyboard-focus-routing`, `main-menu-search-selection`, `theme-config-preferences`, and `testing-quality-gates`.
- This is a real `$parallel-goal-oracle-devtools` implementation worker, not read-only research.
- Use the required loop: gather current source/runtime data, consult Oracle before implementation, read full Oracle output, implement, verify, commit, cleanup.
- Use Oracle/session slug `snippet-form-multiline-0521`.
- Inspect `git status --short --untracked-files=all` before editing.
- IMPORTANT ownership gate: another active worker is handling `snippet-form-font-size-0521` and may edit menu-syntax form/render/theme files. You may gather data and run Oracle now, but do not edit overlapping files until the parent confirms the font-size worker is committed/closed or grants ownership expansion. If you need to edit files owned by that worker, report the claimed files and wait.
- Known unrelated untracked files may include `memories/conversations/*`; do not touch them.
- Use `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh` for all cargo invocations; do not use bare cargo.

Functional target:
- `;snippet` form's primary `Snippet` field supports multiline input and preserves newlines in the captured snippet body.
- Keyboard behavior is coherent: ordinary text entry works, multiline entry has a discoverable/consistent line-break path based on the existing agent chat input pattern, and submission/focus routing still works.
- Audit other capture form fields. Likely multiline candidates include body/description-like fields; avoid broad changes to URL/name/keyword/tag/single-value metadata.
- Placeholder text should not repeat the field key. For labels like `name *`, placeholder should be `Snippet name` or similar, not `name:Snippet name`; `keyword` should not show `keyword:value`; `description` should not show `description:value`.
- Keep styling consistent with the active theme and recent required-label/font-size fixes.

Verification expectations:
- DevTools red data for current `;snippet` form: show single-line limitation or current multiline failure, and capture current placeholders with duplicated keys.
- Oracle planning before edits using red receipts and relevant source/skill context.
- Focused source tests/audits for multiline field kind/rendering, placeholder cleanup, and any audited multiline candidate decisions.
- Runtime DevTools green proof: `;snippet` form can accept multiline snippet body and state/serialized capture preserves newline; placeholders no longer include duplicate `key:` prefixes; focus/tab/submission semantics remain intact.
- Run focused checks through `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh`, plus `git diff --check`.
- Commit only verified owned files and report commit hash, proof paths, skipped checks, and cleanup status.

# Oracle verification plan for isolated-devtools-agent-bootstrap.

## Round 24

### You

/goal Fix the dictation window background so it stays in sync with the main window theme/material/transparency/vibrancy/background values.

User report:
- Screenshot shows the dictation window background no longer matches the main window background/material.
- All windows should stay perfectly in sync with shared theme/material/transparency/vibrancy values.

Hard requirements:
- Read AGENTS.md and relevant repo-local skills before editing. Likely skills: `dictation-media`, `theme-config-preferences`, `gpui-ui-foundation`, `platform-windowing-macos`, `script-kit-devtools`, and `testing-quality-gates`.
- This is a real `$parallel-goal-oracle-devtools` implementation worker, not read-only research.
- Use the required loop: gather current runtime/source data, consult Oracle before implementation, read full Oracle output, implement, verify, commit, cleanup.
- Use Oracle/session slug `dictation-background-sync-0521`.
- Inspect `git status --short --untracked-files=all` before editing.
- Active form workers may be touching `src/render_script_list/mod.rs` and menu-syntax form files. Avoid those unless absolutely required; this task should likely own dictation/window/theme/material rendering files.
- Known unrelated untracked files may include `memories/conversations/*`; do not touch them.
- Use `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh` for all cargo invocations; do not use bare cargo.

Functional target:
- Dictation window background uses the same shared theme/material/transparency/vibrancy/background values as the main window.
- Avoid duplicating color/material constants in dictation-specific code.
- If a shared window/background helper exists, use it. If not, add the narrowest shared helper in the existing theme/window style pattern and migrate dictation to it.
- Preserve dictation UI layout and controls.

Verification expectations:
- DevTools/runtime red receipt comparing dictation window and main window background/material state if available, or source/runtime screenshot proof if state lacks material fields.
- Oracle planning before edits using red receipts and relevant source/skill context.
- Focused source tests/audits proving dictation uses shared background/material theme values rather than its own stale constants.
- Runtime green proof: dictation window and main window background/material values are synchronized, with screenshot/state receipts.
- Run focused checks via `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh`, plus `git diff --check`.
- Commit only verified owned files and report commit hash, proof paths, skipped checks, and cleanup status.

Implemented and committed: `e7687fe440c6` (`fix: stabilize snippet form typography`).

Changed [src/render_script_list/mod.rs](/Users/johnlindquist/dev/script-kit-gpui/src/render_script_list/mod.rs) so menu-syntax capture form value/placeholder typography now comes from `get_tokens(design_variant).typography().font_size_sm` with token-owned line height. Added a source audit in [tests/source_audits/menu_syntax_handler_form_contract.rs](/Users/johnlindquist/dev/script-kit-gpui/tests/source_audits/menu_syntax_handler_form_contract.rs) to prevent regressions to the old focused `ui_size + 2.0` override or ad hoc fallback size.

Canonical bootstrap for isolated Script Kit GPUI DevTools sessions: one front
door script (classify, verify-script, start, prove, cleanup), repo-level helpers
under scripts/agentic/, bounded timeouts, JSON stdout, progress stderr. Use when
agents need parallel DevTools workers, isolated runtime proof, SK_VERIFY script
gates, or dev-watch reuse without starting a second GPUI under ./dev.sh.

One Next Slice
- Add/narrow a shared helper for main-window-matched themed window background RGBA, using existing theme opacity/material semantics rather than dictation constants.
- Migrate dictation render and preview to that shared helper while preserving existing gradient layers and layout.
- Add a focused source audit proving dictation uses the shared background helper and native shared vibrancy/material path.

## Verification Plan
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --test source_audits dictation_window_uses_shared_theme_background_and_material -- --nocapture` or nearest focused source audit target.
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh check --lib` if the touched Rust requires compilation beyond the audit.
- `git diff --check`.
- Runtime green proof through DevTools front door if feasible; otherwise record missing style/material primitive and use source/runtime screenshot proof fallback.
EOF

## Round 35

### You

/goal resume

Oracle completed for `snippet-multiline-newline-crash`; read the full output at `~/.oracle/sessions/snippet-multiline-newline-crash/output.log` and implement now.

Plan summary:
- Root cause: newline-containing snippet body is valid state, but leaks into plain GPUI text children. GPUI text layout panics on `\n` with `text argument should not contain newlines`.
- Claim only `src/render_script_list/mod.rs` and `tests/source_audits/menu_syntax_handler_form_contract.rs`.
- Add display-only sanitizer such as `menu_syntax_single_line_text_for_gpui` that replaces `\n`/`\r` with spaces.
- Apply only to plain text render paths: `render_menu_syntax_hint_row`, `render_menu_syntax_fragment_preview_row`, and the fallback `display_value` in `render_menu_syntax_form_field`.
- Do not sanitize or mutate `MenuSyntaxFormFieldSnapshot.value`, `menu_syntax_form_draft_value`, `InputState` value, `CaptureInvocation.body`, or serialized snippet body.

Required verification:
- focused helper/source-audit test(s)
- existing `editing_snippet_body_preserves_internal_newlines`
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh check --lib`
- `git diff --check`
- green DevTools proof of real path: type prefix in `;snippet` body, Shift+Enter/newline, type suffix, app alive, body value preserves newline, no panic/log `text argument should not contain newlines`.

Commit the verified slice and report commit hash/proof/cleanup.

### Assistant

Implemented and committed: `f3da3eadd Use popup for dictation microphone picker`.

What changed:
- Added dictation-owned attached popup: `src/dictation/microphone_popup_window.rs`
- Rewired overlay mic button in `src/dictation/window.rs` from cycle-to-next-mic to opening the attached `PromptPopup`
- Selection now persists through shared dictation device helpers and bumps passive dictation state generation
- Overlay mic label now uses the selected mic display label with normal ellipsized text instead of the old 8-char keycap
- Added PromptPopup automation routing and semantic collector rows for the dictation mic popup
- Added focused source-contract tests for popup wiring, safe semantic IDs, and label behavior

Verification:
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh check --lib` green
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --test dictation_overlay_focus_hide_contract` green
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --test dictation_microphone_popup_contract` green
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --test sdk_automation_semantic_ids` green
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --test actions select_microphone` green
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh build --bin script-kit-gpui` green
- `git diff --check` green

Oracle and DevTools receipts:
- Oracle session: `~/.oracle/sessions/dictation-mic-trigger-popup-2/output.log`
- Primary green DevTools receipt: `/tmp/script-kit-gpui-proofs/dictation-mic-green-3-after-select.json`
- Before receipt: `/tmp/script-kit-gpui-proofs/dictation-mic-green-3-before.json`
- Selection elements receipt: `/tmp/script-kit-gpui-proofs/dictation-mic-green-3-elements-before-select.json`

Runtime proof result:
- Before selection: `configured=false`, fingerprint `null`, generation `1`
- After selection: `configured=true`, fingerprint `fnv1a64:ecb307f9ac8c5fe2`, generation `2`
- No microphone capture, no system settings, no TCC mutation.

Limitations / remaining risk:
- DevTools currently has no passive primitive to open the live recording overlay mic popup without starting capture, so I did not start real mic capture. Popup-window/label behavior is covered by source-contract tests; selected-device mutation is proven by no-capture runtime state.
- Passive dictation inspect redacts labels/raw IDs. The legacy settings MiniPrompt `getElements` still exposes raw device values; the new dictation popup automation contract uses safe row IDs.
- Left unrelated untracked `memories/conversations/*` files untouched.
- Cleaned up launched sessions: `dictation-mic-red`, `dictation-mic-green-2`, `dictation-mic-green-3`.

## Round 41

### You

/goal resume

Oracle completed for `snippet-form-size-scroll`; read the full output at `~/.oracle/sessions/snippet-form-size-scroll/output.log` and implement now.

Plan summary:
- Root cause for too-small fonts: `render_script_list` uses menu-syntax-local small typography (`font_size_sm`) and 11px labels instead of theme/form-field scale.
- Root cause for Shift+Enter jump: live InputEvent::Change serializes back to filter text and then unconditionally re-focuses/re-selects the already-focused field through `focus_menu_syntax_form_input_at`, causing GPUI input scroll-to-end math to briefly hide the first line.

Claimed files:
- `src/render_script_list/mod.rs`
- `src/app_impl/menu_syntax_main_hint.rs`
- `tests/source_audits/menu_syntax_handler_form_contract.rs`
Do not touch dictation files, prompt_handler, automation collector, or GPUI vendor internals unless first patch remains red and you report why.

Implementation requirements:
- Replace the snippet/form value typography helper with theme/form-field scale: label roughly `ui_size - 2`, value/placeholder roughly `ui_size + 2`, theme-derived line heights, no raw 11px labels.
- Apply sizing consistently to labels, live input value, placeholder/fallback text, and field heights.
- For multiline snippet body, use `auto_grow(2, 6)` and render min height as two rows, max six rows.
- Skip `focus_menu_syntax_form_input_at` / selection reset when `update_menu_syntax_form_field` is called from the already-focused live input change path (`menu_syntax_form_syncing_from_input` should distinguish this). Preserve focus/reveal for Tab traversal, protocol/simulateKey edits, suggestions, and non-focused field updates.
- Preserve prior snippet behavior: multiline state/newline serialization and display-only newline sanitization from commits `5e7559373` and `b42d047ad`.

Verification expected:
- updated source audit for theme/form sizing and no unconditional live-input refocus
- `editing_snippet_body_preserves_internal_newlines`
- `editing_snippet_metadata_keeps_body_after_delimiter`
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh check --lib` or `check --bin script-kit-gpui` if source supports it
- `git diff --check`
- green DevTools proof/screenshot: `;snippet`, body focused, type `first`, Shift+Enter, first line remains visible/no jump, body value preserves `first\n`, sizing is theme-consistent and stable focused/unfocused.

Commit verified slice and report commit hash/proof/cleanup.

Interrupt the previous broad crash/error audit. New user request supersedes it.

New active goal in /Users/johnlindquist/dev/script-kit-gpui:
`$parallel-goal-oracle-devtools read the recent commits about ;snippet and do an audit for the other forms to make sure they're following the same patterns and using theme-established values. Extracting anything hard-coded into reusable tokens shared throughout the app`

Required workflow still applies:
- Use parallel-goal-oracle-devtools: DevTools/source data -> Oracle browser consult before edits -> implement -> verify with tests/DevTools where applicable -> commit.
- Use goal-subagent expectations: stay on task until verified and committed, not just research.
- Load relevant skills: oracle-packx, script-kit-devtools, testing-quality-gates, prompt-runtime, gpui-ui-foundation, theme-config-preferences, keyboard-focus-routing as needed.
- Preserve unrelated work; do not revert anything; use `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh` for cargo.

Concrete parent data gathered for new scope:
- Recent snippet/form commits:
  - `11d398e80 fix: stabilize snippet form sizing and newline scroll`
  - `b42d047ad fix: sanitize multiline snippet render text`
  - `5e7559373 fix: support multiline snippet capture body`
  - `e7687fe44 fix: stabilize snippet form typography`
  - `d69302f2b Fix capture form required labels and scroll reveal`
  - `2b154f161 Render snippet metadata as key value markdown`
  - `2f20f166b Add snippet scriptlet markdown capture`
  - adjacent handler-form commits: `49fc54ced`, `d1238353a`, `38e38db2b`, `6400ad426`, `965026716`, `54c691163`, `a29d9281e`, `becda0bc2`.
- Worktree has only pre-existing untracked `memories/conversations/*` files.
- User wants the same patterns applied across other forms: consistent theme-established font/placeholder/label/value sizing, multiline behavior where needed, no hard-coded magic dimensions when reusable tokens should exist, shared tokens/components across the app.

Your implementation target:
1. Audit current handler/capture forms and general form field renderers against the recent `;snippet` changes.
2. Ask Oracle for exact extraction and implementation plan. Suggested slug: `snippet-form-token-audit`.
3. Implement reusable theme-backed form tokens or shared helpers where source truth supports it, and migrate obvious hard-coded form values used by handler forms/general fields. Keep scope shippable and avoid broad cosmetic churn.
4. Add/adjust source-contract tests proving other forms follow the snippet token patterns and no regressions in required-field marking, placeholders, multiline sizing, and focus/scroll behavior.
5. Verify with focused tests, `check --lib` if needed, `git diff --check`, and DevTools proof for at least one non-snippet form if runtime-observable.
6. Commit the verified slice. Commit body must mention Oracle slug and primary proof/receipt.

Report stage updates and final: Oracle session path, files changed, commit hash, exact proof, skipped checks, cleanup.

fn quote_token_value(value: &str) -> String {

}

fn fence_content<'a>(section: &'a str, lang: &str) -> Option<&'a str> {
let marker = format!("```{lang}");
let start = section.find(&marker)?;
let after_marker = start + marker.len();
let content_start = section[after_marker..].find('\n')? + after_marker + 1;
let content_end = section[content_start..].find("\n```")? + content_start;

## Round 56

### You

/goal Fix the two ;snippet form regressions shown by the user screenshot: (1) placeholder text renders too large before focus and shrinks after focus; keep placeholder/font sizing stable and theme-owned, matching the focused size. (2) Shift+Enter must insert a newline in the multiline Snippet field instead of submitting or failing.

Hard requirements:
- Read AGENTS.md and relevant repo-local skills before editing. Use gpui-ui-foundation, keyboard-focus-routing, prompt-runtime/menu-syntax form context, script-kit-devtools, oracle-packx, and testing-quality-gates as applicable.
- Use the required inner loop: gather runtime/source data, consult Oracle before implementation, implement the first shippable slice, verify with focused tests and DevTools where possible, commit, and clean up.
- You are an implementation worker, not a read-only investigator. Produce a verified task-scoped commit unless a real blocker prevents it.
- Use a unique session/process slug: snippet-form-newline-font-0522.
- You are not alone in the codebase. Do not revert edits made by others; adjust around current HEAD and nearby committed work.
- Ownership: form field rendering/input behavior only. Likely files include src/components/form_fields/*, src/app_impl/menu_syntax_main_hint.rs, src/render_script_list/mod.rs, and focused tests/source audits. The parent owns snippet keyword trigger loading/keyword_manager. If you need src/keyword_manager/mod.rs or scriptlet loader files, stop and report before editing.
- Expect DevTools runs to be interrupted by the user typing, opening another popover, or dismissing the global window; classify and retry these separately from product failures.
- Clean up every DevTools/app/helper process you launch.
- Do not touch unrelated dirty work or untracked memories/conversations files.
- Report changed files, commit hash, Oracle session slug, proof commands/receipts, skipped verification, and cleanup status.

## Round 57

### You

/goal Figure out and implement high-quality Actions menu entries for the ;snippet form flow.

User goal: In the ;snippet form, identify useful quality actions we can add to the Actions menu, then implement the first shippable set. Think through actual snippet authoring/editing workflows: create/update/delete, copy/open/reveal backing snippets.md, preview generated markdown, insert common fields, maybe test expansion text, etc. Do not add noisy actions; choose actions that are coherent with existing app patterns.

Hard requirements:
- Read AGENTS.md and relevant repo-local skills before editing. Use actions-popups, prompt-runtime/menu-syntax form context, gpui-ui-foundation, script-kit-devtools, oracle-packx, and testing-quality-gates as applicable.
- Use the required inner loop: gather runtime/source data, consult Oracle before implementation, implement the first shippable slice, verify with focused tests and DevTools where possible, commit, and clean up.
- You are an implementation worker, not a read-only investigator. Produce a verified task-scoped commit unless a real blocker prevents it.
- Use a unique session/process slug: snippet-actions-menu-0522.
- You are not alone in the codebase. Do not revert edits made by others; adjust around current HEAD and nearby committed work.
- Ownership: ;snippet Actions menu/action registration and execution surfaces. Likely files include menu_syntax, actions/popup definitions, app_execute/menu_syntax_builtin_execution.rs, render/menu-syntax form integration, and focused tests. Parent owns keyword_manager/scriptlet loading. Pauli owns form field font/newline behavior. If you need src/components/form_fields/* or src/keyword_manager/mod.rs, stop and report before editing.
- Expect DevTools runs to be interrupted by the user typing, opening another popover, or dismissing the global window; classify and retry these separately from product failures.
- Clean up every DevTools/app/helper process you launch.
- Do not touch unrelated dirty work or untracked memories/conversations files.
- Report changed files, commit hash, Oracle session slug, proof commands/receipts, skipped verification, and cleanup status.

### You

/goal Remove opaque background colors in Theme Designer and any adjacent surfaces that use the same hard-coded card/field backgrounds, aligning them with the ;snippet form field backgrounds through shared theme tokens.

User supplied screenshot: Theme Designer right-side cards are opaque black while surrounding window/theme material is translucent/consistent. Goal is consistency: extract/reuse theme tokens for field/card backgrounds instead of ad hoc opaque colors.

Hard requirements:
- Read AGENTS.md and relevant repo-local skills before editing: theme-config-preferences, gpui-ui-foundation, storybook-design if useful, script-kit-devtools, oracle-packx, and testing-quality-gates.
- Use the required loop: gather source/runtime data, consult Oracle before implementation, implement first shippable slice, verify with focused tests and DevTools/runtime/screenshot proof where possible, commit, clean up.
- Use session slug: theme-designer-field-backgrounds-0522.
- You are not alone in the codebase. Do not revert others' work.
- Ownership: Theme Designer/background token work. Likely files include theme designer render modules, shared design/theme token modules, and focused tests/source audits. Parent owns src/keyword_manager/mod.rs. Anscombe owns ;snippet form font/newline behavior. Faraday owns ;snippet Actions menu behavior. If you need src/components/form_fields/*, src/menu_syntax/*, or files currently dirty from other workers, stop and report before editing or wait until their commits land.
- Preserve untracked memories/conversations files.
- Report changed files, commit hash, Oracle session slug, proof commands/receipts/screenshots, skipped verification, and cleanup status.

### Assistant

Implemented and committed the first shippable `;snippet` Actions menu slice.

Commit: `a38227d28 Add snippet form actions menu entries`  
Goal usage: `324,929` tokens, about `19m 12s`.

Changed files:
- `src/menu_syntax/actions.rs`
- `src/menu_syntax/action_effects.rs`
- `src/scriptlets/snippet_markdown_store.rs`
- `tests/menu_syntax_actions_ordering_pin.rs`
- `tests/menu_syntax_run11_attacker_pass32_action_effects.rs`

What shipped:
- `Cancel without saving`
- `Copy Snippet Command`
- `Insert Name Field`
- `Insert Keyword Field`
- `Copy Generated Markdown`
- `Copy snippets.md Path`
- `Prepare Update for Selected Snippet`
- `Prepare Delete for Selected Snippet`

Delete/update actions are non-destructive: they only rewrite the filter text to the relevant `;snippet update/delete @snippet:<id>` command. Open/reveal/test-expansion were deliberately left out of this slice because they require broader side-effect/runtime behavior.

Oracle:
- Session slug: `snippet-actions-menu-0522`
- Oracle completed and recommended the same core direction: snippet-specific rows, pure `SetFilterText`/`WriteClipboard` effects, destructive behavior only as prepared text, and defer open/reveal.

Proof:
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --test menu_syntax_actions_ordering_pin -- --nocapture`
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --lib menu_syntax::actions -- --nocapture`
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --lib menu_syntax::action_effects -- --nocapture`
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --lib scriptlets::snippet_markdown_store -- --nocapture`
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --test source_audits menu_syntax_handler_form_contract -- --nocapture`
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --test menu_syntax_run11_attacker_pass32_action_effects -- --nocapture`

# agent-owned CARGO_TARGET_DIR so it does not contend on `target/.cargo-lock`
# with the always-on `./dev.sh` cargo-watch loop.
#
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --lib context_picker

### Assistant

Both managed subagents are now closed.

Landed commits:

- `402eabd0a` `fix: stabilize snippet form text and newline input`
  - Keeps `;snippet` placeholder/value text sizing stable through shared form metrics.
  - Syncs Shift+Enter newline insertion from the live multiline input state.

- `bc18faba5` `Align Theme Designer card backgrounds`
  - Adds shared whisper surface/border chrome tokens.
  - Uses those tokens for Theme Designer customize cards and management buttons instead of opaque panel backgrounds.

Verification completed in a clean temporary worktree with only these slices applied:

- `git diff --check` for the owned files

### Assistant

You’re right. The main menu footer is on a different path: `HintStrip` / `render_universal_prompt_hint_strip_clickable_with_primary_label`, not `PromptFooter`.

I applied the same `6px` optical nudge to the native hint-strip return SVG in [hint_strip.rs](/Users/johnlindquist/dev/script-kit-gpui/src/components/hint_strip.rs). That should cover the main menu footer’s `↵ Run` and `⌘↵ Agent` icons.

Verification passed:

- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --lib hint_strip::inline_shortcut_tests::return_icon_gets_footer_optical_nudge`
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --lib prompt_footer::tests::test_return_key_glyph_gets_special_optical_nudge`

## Round 70

### You

[Image #1] 2 bugs:
1. The scrolling in the snippet form isn't bring the field completly into view
2. shift+return in the multiline text field It is sending the cursor back to the main input. I think it's moving the cursor to a new line, but then automatically sending it back to the main. The user scenario that's broken is going to the multi-line input, pressing Shift+Enter, and then typing. Instead of typing into the multi-line input, it's jumping back to the main input.

### Assistant

Fixed both paths in [menu_syntax_main_hint.rs](/Users/johnlindquist/dev/script-kit-gpui/src/app_impl/menu_syntax_main_hint.rs):

- Form scroll reveal now uses a smaller effective viewport and larger margin, so tabbed fields are brought fully into view above the footer.
- Focus from live multiline field updates is restored synchronously and again on the next deferred window tick, so serialized `;snippet ...` filter sync can’t steal focus back to the main input after Shift+Return.
- Handler form printable key routing now also consumes single-character keys when `key_char` is missing, which closes the same “typed into main input instead of form field” path in automation/protocol delivery.

Updated the source audit in [menu_syntax_handler_form_contract.rs](/Users/johnlindquist/dev/script-kit-gpui/tests/source_audits/menu_syntax_handler_form_contract.rs).

Verification:
- `git diff --check -- src/app_impl/menu_syntax_main_hint.rs tests/source_audits/menu_syntax_handler_form_contract.rs`
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --test source_audits menu_syntax_handler_form_contract -- --nocapture` passed: 22/22 tests.
- DevTools layout proof showed a focused lower form field fully visible after tabbing (`focusedSemanticId: handler-form:snippet:kv:enter`, `fullyVisible: true`, `scrollOffsetY: 400`).

DevTools still blocks Shift+Enter dispatch as submit-like before it reaches the app, so that specific key path is covered by the source-contract test rather than a live `act.key` receipt.

### Assistant

Done. Footer keycap borders now use the same theme opacity values as main menu list rows:

- idle/footer keycap border: `theme.opacity.hover`
- selected footer keycap border: `theme.opacity.selected`

This is wired through [footer_chrome.rs](/Users/johnlindquist/dev/script-kit-gpui/src/components/footer_chrome.rs:41) and applied to the native footer in [footer_popup.rs](/Users/johnlindquist/dev/script-kit-gpui/src/footer_popup.rs:1223). I also added a test that compares the footer keycap alpha directly against `ListItemColors`.

Verified:
- `git diff --check -- src/components/footer_chrome.rs src/footer_popup.rs`
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --lib components::footer_chrome::tests -- --nocapture`
- `./scripts/agentic/agent-cargo.sh test --lib footer_popup::footer_layout_tests -- --nocapture`
